nodes.text: Expense and Travel Policy
nodes.section_path: Expense and Travel Policy

nodes.text: Aurora Retail Holdings
nodes.section_path: Aurora Retail Holdings

nodes.text: Effective Date: January 1, 2024
nodes.section_path: Effective Date: January 1, 2024

nodes.text: Owner: Corporate Finance – Accounts Payable
nodes.section_path: Owner: Corporate Finance – Accounts Payable

nodes.text: 1. Purpose and Scope
nodes.section_path: 1. Purpose and Scope

nodes.text: 1.1 The purpose of this Expense and Travel Policy is to provide clear guidelines for incurring, approving, and reimbursing business expenses on behalf of Aurora Retail Holdings (“the Company”).
nodes.section_path: 1.1 The purpose of this Expense and Travel Policy is to provide clear guidelines for incurring, approving, and reimbursing business expenses on behalf of Aurora Retail Holdings (“the Company”).

nodes.text: 1.2 The policy applies to all employees, contractors, and officers who incur expenses that will be charged to the Company.
nodes.section_path: 1.2 The policy applies to all employees, contractors, and officers who incur expenses that will be charged to the Company.

nodes.text: 1.3 The objectives are to:
nodes.section_path: 1.3 The objectives are to:

nodes.text: a) Ensure expenses are reasonable, necessary, and directly related to Company business;
nodes.section_path: 1.3 The objectives are to:

nodes.text: b) Promote consistent treatment across departments; and
nodes.section_path: 1.3 The objectives are to:

nodes.text: c) Facilitate timely and accurate reimbursement and accounting.
nodes.section_path: c) Facilitate timely and accurate reimbursement and accounting.

nodes.text: 2. Allowable vs. Non‑Allowable Expenses
nodes.section_path: 2. Allowable vs. Non‑Allowable Expenses

nodes.text: 2.1 Allowable expenses
nodes.section_path: 2.1 Allowable expenses

nodes.text: 2.1.1 Examples of allowable business expenses include:
nodes.section_path: 2.1.1 Examples of allowable business expenses include:

nodes.text: a) Transportation for business travel (air, rail, rideshare, mileage for personal vehicles)
nodes.section_path: a) Transportation for business travel (air, rail, rideshare, mileage for personal vehicles)

nodes.text: b) Lodging during approved business trips
nodes.section_path: a) Transportation for business travel (air, rail, rideshare, mileage for personal vehicles)

nodes.text: c) Meals and reasonable incidentals while traveling
nodes.section_path: a) Transportation for business travel (air, rail, rideshare, mileage for personal vehicles)

nodes.text: d) Registration fees for approved conferences or training
nodes.section_path: a) Transportation for business travel (air, rail, rideshare, mileage for personal vehicles)

nodes.text: e) Modest client entertainment where business is discussed
nodes.section_path: a) Transportation for business travel (air, rail, rideshare, mileage for personal vehicles)

nodes.text: 2.1.2 All allowable expenses must:
nodes.section_path: 2.1.2 All allowable expenses must:

nodes.text: a) Have a clear business purpose;
nodes.section_path: 2.1.2 All allowable expenses must:

nodes.text: b) Be supported by itemized receipts; and
nodes.section_path: 2.1.2 All allowable expenses must:

nodes.text: c) Comply with this policy and any local regulations.
nodes.section_path: c) Comply with this policy and any local regulations.

nodes.text: 2.2 Non‑allowable expenses
nodes.section_path: 2.2 Non‑allowable expenses

nodes.text: 2.2.1 The following are typically not reimbursable:
nodes.section_path: 2.2.1 The following are typically not reimbursable:

nodes.text: a) Personal travel or vacation days added to a business trip (incremental cost)
nodes.section_path: 2.2.1 The following are typically not reimbursable:

nodes.text: b) Luxury or premium upgrades (e.g., first‑class air, luxury hotels) without pre‑approval
nodes.section_path: b) Luxury or premium upgrades (e.g., first‑class air, luxury hotels) without pre‑approval

nodes.text: c) Fines or penalties (e.g., parking tickets)
nodes.section_path: c) Fines or penalties (e.g., parking tickets)

nodes.text: d) Alcohol in excess of modest, business‑appropriate consumption
nodes.section_path: d) Alcohol in excess of modest, business‑appropriate consumption

nodes.text: e) Family member travel, unless pre‑approved in writing by an Executive
nodes.section_path: d) Alcohol in excess of modest, business‑appropriate consumption

nodes.text: 2.2.2 If employees are unsure whether an expense is allowable, they should consult their manager or Accounts Payable before incurring the expense.
nodes.section_path: 2.2.2 If employees are unsure whether an expense is allowable, they should consult their manager or Accounts Payable before incurring the expense.

nodes.text: 3. Travel Booking Rules
nodes.section_path: 3. Travel Booking Rules

nodes.text: 3.1 General principles
nodes.section_path: 3.1 General principles

nodes.text: 3.1.1 Employees should book travel as early as practical to secure competitive rates.
nodes.section_path: 3.1.1 Employees should book travel as early as practical to secure competitive rates.

nodes.text: 3.1.2 Economy class is the standard for air and rail travel. Premium cabins may be approved for:
nodes.section_path: 3.1.2 Economy class is the standard for air and rail travel. Premium cabins may be approved for:

nodes.text: a) Flights exceeding 8 hours; or
nodes.section_path: 3.1.2 Economy class is the standard for air and rail travel. Premium cabins may be approved for:

nodes.text: b) Medical reasons documented with HR; or
nodes.section_path: 3.1.2 Economy class is the standard for air and rail travel. Premium cabins may be approved for:

nodes.text: c) Executive‑level travel where required for security or productivity.
nodes.section_path: 3.1.2 Economy class is the standard for air and rail travel. Premium cabins may be approved for:

nodes.text: 3.2 Lodging
nodes.section_path: 3.2 Lodging

nodes.text: 3.2.1 Employees should book mid‑range business hotels near the work location or event.
nodes.section_path: 3.2.1 Employees should book mid‑range business hotels near the work location or event.

nodes.text: 3.2.2 Hotel upgrades (e.g., suites, club floors) are not reimbursable unless:
nodes.section_path: 3.2.2 Hotel upgrades (e.g., suites, club floors) are not reimbursable unless:

nodes.text: a) The upgraded room is the only available option; or
nodes.section_path: 3.2.2 Hotel upgrades (e.g., suites, club floors) are not reimbursable unless:

nodes.text: b) Pre‑approved by a VP or above for client hosting.
nodes.section_path: 3.2.2 Hotel upgrades (e.g., suites, club floors) are not reimbursable unless:

nodes.text: 3.2.3 All incidental hotel charges (room service, mini‑bar, movies) must be itemized. Personal entertainment costs are not reimbursable.
nodes.section_path: 3.2.3 All incidental hotel charges (room service, mini‑bar, movies) must be itemized. Personal entertainment costs are not reimbursable.

nodes.text: 3.3 Ground transportation
nodes.section_path: 3.3 Ground transportation

nodes.text: 3.3.1 Employees should choose the most cost‑effective option considering safety and time:
nodes.section_path: 3.3.1 Employees should choose the most cost‑effective option considering safety and time:

nodes.text: a) Public transit
nodes.section_path: a) Public transit

nodes.text: b) Rideshare or taxis
nodes.section_path: b) Rideshare or taxis

nodes.text: c) Rental cars where appropriate for the location and itinerary
nodes.section_path: c) Rental cars where appropriate for the location and itinerary

nodes.text: 3.3.2 When renting a car:
nodes.section_path: 3.3.2 When renting a car:

nodes.text: a) Compact or mid‑size is the standard class
nodes.section_path: 3.3.2 When renting a car:

nodes.text: b) Additional insurance should only be purchased where required by local law or if personal coverage is not available
nodes.section_path: 3.3.2 When renting a car:

nodes.text: 4. Reimbursement Process and Timelines
nodes.section_path: 4. Reimbursement Process and Timelines

nodes.text: 4.1 Expense submission
nodes.section_path: 4.1 Expense submission

nodes.text: 4.1.1 Employees must submit expense reports within 30 days of incurring the expense or returning from a trip.
nodes.section_path: 4.1.1 Employees must submit expense reports within 30 days of incurring the expense or returning from a trip.

nodes.text: 4.1.2 Expense reports must include:
nodes.section_path: 4.1.2 Expense reports must include:

nodes.text: a) Date and description of each expense
nodes.section_path: a) Date and description of each expense

nodes.text: b) Business purpose
nodes.section_path: b) Business purpose

nodes.text: c) Project or cost center code
nodes.section_path: c) Project or cost center code

nodes.text: d) Scanned itemized receipts for all expenses greater than or equal to USD 25
nodes.section_path: c) Project or cost center code

nodes.text: 4.2 Payment timing
nodes.section_path: 4.2 Payment timing

nodes.text: 4.2.1 Approved expense reports will be reimbursed via payroll or direct deposit within 10 business days of final approval.
nodes.section_path: 4.2.1 Approved expense reports will be reimbursed via payroll or direct deposit within 10 business days of final approval.

nodes.text: 4.2.2 Reimbursement delays due to missing documentation are the responsibility of the submitter.
nodes.section_path: 4.2.2 Reimbursement delays due to missing documentation are the responsibility of the submitter.

nodes.text: 4.3 Currency and exchange rates
nodes.section_path: 4.3 Currency and exchange rates

nodes.text: 4.3.1 Foreign currency expenses should be converted using:
nodes.section_path: 4.3.1 Foreign currency expenses should be converted using:

nodes.text: a) The rate on the credit card statement; or
nodes.section_path: a) The rate on the credit card statement; or

nodes.text: b) A reasonable published rate on the date of the transaction.
nodes.section_path: a) The rate on the credit card statement; or

nodes.text: 4.3.2 Employees must not manipulate exchange rates to increase reimbursement.
nodes.section_path: 4.3.2 Employees must not manipulate exchange rates to increase reimbursement.

nodes.text: 5. Approval Workflow
nodes.section_path: 5. Approval Workflow

nodes.text: 5.1 Manager approval
nodes.section_path: 5.1 Manager approval

nodes.text: 5.1.1 All expense reports must be reviewed and approved by the employee’s direct manager or designated approver.
nodes.section_path: 5.1.1 All expense reports must be reviewed and approved by the employee’s direct manager or designated approver.

nodes.text: 5.1.2 Managers are responsible for verifying:
nodes.section_path: 5.1.2 Managers are responsible for verifying:

nodes.text: a) Business relevance of the expense
nodes.section_path: a) Business relevance of the expense

nodes.text: b) Compliance with this policy
nodes.section_path: b) Compliance with this policy

nodes.text: c) Correct coding to cost centers or projects
nodes.section_path: b) Compliance with this policy

nodes.text: 5.2 Additional approvals
nodes.section_path: 5.2 Additional approvals

nodes.text: 5.2.1 The following require pre‑approval from a VP or above:
nodes.section_path: 5.2.1 The following require pre‑approval from a VP or above:

nodes.text: a) International travel
nodes.section_path: a) International travel

nodes.text: b) Conference attendance with a total cost exceeding USD 5,000
nodes.section_path: a) International travel

nodes.text: c) Client entertainment expected to exceed USD 1,000
nodes.section_path: a) International travel

nodes.text: 5.2.2 Exceptions for non‑allowable expenses require written approval from both:
nodes.section_path: 5.2.2 Exceptions for non‑allowable expenses require written approval from both:

nodes.text: a) The relevant VP; and
nodes.section_path: a) The relevant VP; and

nodes.text: b) The Controller or their delegate.
nodes.section_path: b) The Controller or their delegate.

nodes.text: 6. Policy Exceptions
nodes.section_path: 6. Policy Exceptions

nodes.text: 6.1 Exception process
nodes.section_path: 6.1 Exception process

nodes.text: 6.1.1 When business needs require deviation from this policy (e.g., last‑minute bookings, limited lodging options), employees should:
nodes.section_path: 6.1.1 When business needs require deviation from this policy (e.g., last‑minute bookings, limited lodging options), employees should:

nodes.text: a) Document the reason for the exception; and
nodes.section_path: a) Document the reason for the exception; and

nodes.text: b) Obtain written pre‑approval from their manager or VP where practical.
nodes.section_path: a) Document the reason for the exception; and

nodes.text: 6.1.2 In urgent situations where pre‑approval is not possible, the exception and justification should be documented in the expense report for after‑the‑fact review.
nodes.section_path: 6.1.2 In urgent situations where pre‑approval is not possible, the exception and justification should be documented in the expense report for after‑the‑fact review.

nodes.text: 6.2 Review and enforcement
nodes.section_path: 6.2 Review and enforcement

nodes.text: 6.2.1 Accounts Payable may reject or adjust expenses that do not comply with this policy.
nodes.section_path: 6.2.1 Accounts Payable may reject or adjust expenses that do not comply with this policy.

nodes.text: 6.2.2 Repeated non‑compliance may result in:
nodes.section_path: 6.2.2 Repeated non‑compliance may result in:

nodes.text: a) Delayed reimbursements
nodes.section_path: 6.2.2 Repeated non‑compliance may result in:

nodes.text: b) Escalation to HR
nodes.section_path: b) Escalation to HR

nodes.text: c) Disciplinary action for misuse of Company funds
nodes.section_path: c) Disciplinary action for misuse of Company funds

nodes.text: 7. Policy Review
nodes.section_path: 7. Policy Review

nodes.text: 7.1 This policy will be reviewed annually by Finance and HR.
nodes.section_path: 7.1 This policy will be reviewed annually by Finance and HR.

nodes.text: 7.2 Updates will be communicated via email and posted on the Company intranet.
nodes.section_path: 7.2 Updates will be communicated via email and posted on the Company intranet.
